scene.description: Engine 181 is supplying a portable monitor at a commercial strip fire. The monitor is fed by two 200’ 2½” supply lines. The monitor is flowing 500 GPM at 80 PSI. Use a 10 PSI appliance loss for the portable monitor. No elevation change. What pump pressure should the driver/operator set?

hoses: 200’ 2½” supply line A from Engine 181 to portable monitor
200’ 2½” supply line B from Engine 181 to portable monitor
nozzles: Portable monitor flowing 500 GPM at 80 PSI
appliances: Portable monitor with 10 PSI appliance loss

problems.title: Advanced Portable Monitor Dual Supply
problems.question: Engine 181 is supplying a portable monitor at a commercial strip fire. The monitor is fed by two 200’ 2½” supply lines. The monitor is flowing 500 GPM at 80 PSI. Use a 10 PSI appliance loss for the portable monitor. No elevation change. What pump pressure should the driver/operator set?
formulaBreakdown: PP = FL + NP + elevation + appliance loss
Flow is split between two 2½” supply lines
FL = C × (GPM / 100)^2 × hose length in hundreds
problems.instructorExplanation: This is an advanced portable master stream problem. The driver/operator must split the 500 GPM monitor flow between the two 2½” supply lines before calculating friction loss. Each line carries 250 GPM, so the governing line friction loss is 25 PSI. Add 80 PSI nozzle pressure and 10 PSI appliance loss for a final pump pressure of 115 PSI.
problems.explainMistake: Common mistakes include using 500 GPM for each supply line, adding both friction losses together, forgetting the portable monitor appliance loss, using 50 PSI handline nozzle pressure instead of 80 PSI master stream pressure, or adding elevation when none is present.
